notes: Arturia Synthx V is inspired by the Oberheim Two-Voice and Four-Voice concept — a multi-timbral analog synthesizer comprising up to four independent synthesis voices, each built around dual VCOs, a filter, and its own envelope generator. This architecture allows thick, layered sounds where each voice can carry a different timbre, making it ideal for stacked pads, fat unison leads, and rich polyphonic textures. Per-voice detuning and independent filter cutoffs create organic, wide stereo soundscapes that are difficult to achieve with single-voice instruments. The instrument suits film scoring, ambient music, and any production that demands large-scale, orchestral-weight synthesizer sounds.

Expression.description: Expression — scales output level for dynamics

Portamento Time.description: Portamento time — glide speed between pitches

Filter Cutoff.description: Global filter cutoff — primary brightness control across all active voices

Voice Detune.description: Voice detune spread — detunes individual voices against each other for chorus-like thickening

Attack.description: Envelope attack time — shared across voices for cohesive articulation

Release.description: Envelope release time — tail length after note off

LFO Depth.description: LFO depth — modulation amount applied to pitch or filter

Osc Mix.description: Oscillator mix — balance between the two VCOs within each voice

Reverb.description: Reverb send level — adds space to the wide, layered sound

Chorus.description: Chorus/ensemble depth — additional width and movement

Macro 1.description: Adaptive Macro 1 — label and target change per preset

Macro 2.description: Adaptive Macro 2 — label and target change per preset

Macro 3.description: Adaptive Macro 3 — label and target change per preset

Macro 4.description: Adaptive Macro 4 — label and target change per preset

Macro 5.description: Adaptive Macro 5 — label and target change per preset
